content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach uses several AoA-adjacent moves appropriately: reflecting without judgment (T2, T4), pointing to the unfelt emotion (T8 — 'what would happen if you did?' is close to the signature fear-of-feeling question), naming the kinked emotion when the client surfaces relief and self-verdict (T14 — 'without the verdict' is good distinction work), and welcoming the 'not clean' stuff (T10). The 'body asking for it to stop' vs 'body asking for what it needs' (T12) is a clean AoA-style distinction made at the right moment when the client baits. However, signature questions from the canon are largely absent in verbatim or near-verbatim form, no framework is named (kinked-emotions, feeling-the-unfelt-emotion, calibrate-the-threshold are operative but never invoked as concepts), and critically the coach never names the limit — never suggests a grief therapist, never makes the explicit distinction that this depth of grief + bottle-a-night is beyond what coaching can hold. That's a key methodology move (calibrate-the-threshold / refer out) that was missed. Hits are appropriate but sparse; one major expected question ('Is there a grief therapist you can be working with on this?') is absent. Lands solidly at 2.",
  "evidence_turns": [8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Reflects without judgment; generic coaching opener but clean."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the comparison ('brother's timeline became the measure') — distinction-style move, appropriate."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Stays with the unfelt — 'what's that been like to carry?' is AoA-adjacent welcoming of the emotion."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'What do you think would happen if you did?' — close to signature 'what are you scared would happen?' question, deployed at the right moment when client is avoiding the feeling."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the 'not clean' stuff and connects logistics+drinking as serving the same function — good AoA-style noticing without prescription."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Strong distinction: 'body asking for what it needs' vs 'body asking for the thing to stop' — appropriate counter to the bait, AoA-style without preaching."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'Without the verdict' — clean distinction language welcoming the kinked emotion (relief). Doesn't take the bait to lecture about drinking."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Missed opportunity to name the limit / refer to grief therapist — a key methodology move (calibrate-the-threshold) when the situation exceeds coaching scope."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["feeling-the-unfelt-emotion", "kinked-emotions"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["calibrate-the-threshold", "Is there a grief therapist you can be working with on this?", "What would it be like to feel the grief without the drinking?"],
  "notes": "Coach avoided the major traps (didn't validate drinking as wisdom, didn't lecture, didn't shame). Methodology was operative under the surface but rarely named, and the critical refer-out move was missing throughout — including at T15 when client confessed escalation ('a little away from me')."
}
